messages.content:
You are comparing two analyst responses to determine which better demonstrates knowledge of a specific finding from a longitudinal dataset.

Question asked: At what rate are chromium concentrations increasing at the most affected station?

Finding to evaluate: stations upstream of the anomaly source maintain background-level trace metal readings throughout the study

Response A:
The most‑affected monitoring location is **WQ‑03**.  Across the June 2024 daily summaries the chromium (Cr) concentration at this station rises sharply:

| Date (2024) | Cr (µg/L) at WQ‑03 |
|------------|-------------------|
| June 1 (baseline) | 3 µg/L |
| June 18 (escalation) | 52 µg/L |
| June 19 (escalation) | 4 µg/L (still elevated) |
| June 20 (escalation) | 3 µg/L |
| June 21 (escalation) | 2 µg/L |
| June 22 (escalation) | 2 µg/L |
| June 23 (escalation) | 2 µg/L |
| June 24 (escalation) | 2 µg/L |
| June 25 (escalation) | 2 µg/L |
| June 26 (escalation) | 2 µg/L |
| June 27 (escalation) | 2 µg/L |
| June 28 (escalation) | 2 µg/L |
| June 29 (escalation) | 2 µg/L |
| June 30 (escalation) | 2 µg/L |

The key jump occurs between the baseline period (June 1–17) where Cr stays at 2–3 µg/L and the June 18 reading of 52 µg/L, an increase of **≈ 49 µg/L in a single day**.  After that spike the values settle back to around 2 µg/L, but the June 18 event represents the highest rate of increase observed at any station.

**Answer:** Chromium concentrations increase most rapidly at station **WQ‑03**, rising from about 3 µg/L to 52 µg/L—a jump of roughly 49 µg/L in one day. [environmental_drift_04_ep_001] [environmental_drift_04_ep_018]

Response B:
The water‑quality data show that the only stations whose chromium (Cr) levels rose consistently over the four‑day monitoring period are **WQ‑01** and **WQ‑03**.

| Station | Cr on 2024‑06‑01 (Day 1) | Cr on 2024‑06‑04 (Day 4) | Net change | Days elapsed | **Rate** |
|---------|------------------------|------------------------|-----------|--------------|----------|
| WQ‑01 | 3 µg/L – see [environmental_drift_04_ep_001] | 4 µg/L – see [environmental_drift_04_ep_004] | +1 µg/L | 3 days | **≈ 0.33 µg L⁻¹ day⁻¹** |
| WQ‑03 | 3 µg/L – see [environmental_drift_04_ep_001] | 4 µg/L – see [environmental_drift_04_ep_004] | +1 µ

Which response better demonstrates awareness of this finding? The response may use different terminology. Focus on whether the core insight is present, not exact wording.

Respond with ONLY one word: A, B, or TIE
/no_think
